title: Structural model specification + overidentifying restrictions for the four-factor breaking-point measurement system, on the DP2 CPIS-augmented RE-RUN matrix
status: SPECIFICATION ONLY. No parameter is fit. No factor is scored. Every quantity below is a restriction or an identification ARGUMENT stated so it can FAIL. Estimation is DP4 and is NOT begun here.
reads: build/results/dp2_residual.json ($711bn BIS floor; offshore-hub residency HOLE)
build/audit/dp3_identifiability.md (per-factor verdict ON THE OLD one-column matrix)

what_changed_vs_old_audit.old_support: ONE dense bilateral column (TIC US-Treasury). The audit verdict (F3/F4 NOT IDENTIFIED, separation NOT TESTABLE) was correct ON THAT support: with one issuer column there is no i->j vs i->k split and no multi-issuer holder block.
what_changed_vs_old_audit.new_support: The CPIS re-run supplies a DENSE 13-reporter x 14-counterpart area x area grid (167 observed cells). Confirmed by direct read of matrix_assembled.json: ~12 holders observed per issuer column and ~13 issuer columns observed per holder row. The reallocation axis (i's split across issuers j,k) and the multi-holder dollar-leg block (12 holders of the USA column) BOTH now exist as observed cells. This REFUTES the old audit's data-absence verdict at the level of cell support.
what_changed_vs_old_audit.but_the_decisive_new_constraint: The CPIS block is a SINGLE CROSS-SECTIONAL SNAPSHOT (2025-S1) -- ONE realization of the 13x14 matrix, NOT a panel/time-series. There is no second matrix on disk to form a sample covariance over repeated draws. This moves the F3/F4 question from 'the off-diagonal does not exist' (old) to 'the off-diagonal exists once, so F3 and F4 occupy a rotation-indeterminate subspace whose separation rests ENTIRELY on an a-priori loading restriction -- which is testable only if that restriction is OVERIDENTIFYING.' DP3 must therefore (i) state that restriction, (ii) state its falsifier, (iii) check whether the falsifier-case obtains on THIS single snapshot. This is the substance of STEP 2.

equilibrium_concept.name: Farhi-Maggiori (2018, QJE) reserve-asset multiple-equilibrium model with a global-games (Morris-Shin) equilibrium selection.
equilibrium_concept.object_built: Per CLAUDE.md the breaking point is NOT a scalar/date but three objects: (1) frontier = the fundamentals-gated manifold on which a self-fulfilling reserve-run equilibrium becomes feasible; (2) distance-to-frontier = signed scalar position; (3) hazard = with binding-mode attribution. DP3 specifies the MEASUREMENT SYSTEM that maps observables to the latent state that indexes this manifold; it does NOT locate the frontier (that is downstream and conditional on identification holding).

state_variables.theta: fundamental state of the issuer (US fiscal capacity / safe-asset backing). In Farhi-Maggiori, theta gates whether the run equilibrium exists. NOT directly in the position matrix; entered as an external fundamental index (carried as a model input, not a matrix cell). Marked: theta is NOT a matrix observable -> any claim about its level is out-of-matrix and must be sourced separately (a HOLE for this matrix).
state_variables.x_holders: vector of holders' dollar-leg exposures (the USA issuer column of the CPIS grid + BIS USD funding marginals). Observable.
state_variables.s_split: for each holder, the share split of its portfolio across issuer columns (the CPIS row, normalized). Observable.
state_variables.kappa: convenience-yield / safe-asset premium on the US Treasury leg. PRICE observable, NOT in this STOCK matrix -> F2's price manifestation is a HOLE (see F2).

global_games_selection_operationalized.rule: Selection is operationalized as a THRESHOLD on the fundamental state: a run equilibrium is SELECTED when theta falls below a critical theta*(global-games), where theta* is shifted by the strategic-complementarity strength among holders. The complementarity strength is read off the CROSS-HOLDER co-movement of dollar-leg exposures (F4 loadings on the USA column). HIGHER F4 cross-holder co-movement => stronger complementarity => higher theta* => run feasible at better fundamentals.
global_games_selection_operationalized.what_makes_it_falsifiable: If F4 is NOT separately identified from F3 and from the holder-size/funding-level factor (F1), then the complementarity strength is NOT recoverable from the matrix, and theta*(global-games) cannot be pinned -> the selection step has NO observable input and the breaking-point hazard is NOT IDENTIFIED. This is the load-bearing dependency: the global-games threshold needs F4's cross-holder loading, which STEP 2 tests for identifiability.
global_games_selection_operationalized.not_planted: theta* is NOT defined so that the run 'has not happened' forces non-identification. theta* is a function of an ESTIMABLE complementarity parameter; if the matrix supports estimating it, the threshold is identified and the run's feasibility is a genuine empirical question, answerable in either direction.

F1_funding_stress.definition: Common-level tightness in USD funding across holders/sectors.
observed_quantities_it_loads_on: BIS LBS USD claims/liabilities per-area MARGINALS (I1), 18 named areas (matrix_assembled cross_border_usd_bank_positions_BIS_LBS).
Fed EFA US-internal S2 depository cells (subset of the 585 us_issuer_to_holder cells).
CPIS row TOTALS (holder portfolio size) as a scale control.
F1_funding_stress.mapping_verdict: MAPPED. F1 is a marginal/level factor; the BIS per-area USD marginals + Fed S2 cells carry a genuine cross-section of funding exposure. No pair structure required. Consistent with old audit F1=IDENTIFIED(bounded).
F1_funding_stress.bound: The $711,157.7mn BIS claims-vs-liabilities asymmetry (dp2_residual bis_marginal_asymmetry) is the noise FLOOR on F1's level; it bounds precision but does not break identification of a level factor. CARRIED FORWARD as a HOLE, not resolved.

F2_treasury_despecialization.definition: Erosion of the safe-asset premium / convenience yield on US Treasuries; a factor on the price AND quantity of the safe leg.
observed_quantities_it_loads_on: TIC US-Treasury-by-counterparty column (36 country cells, us_treasury_by_counterparty_TIC) -- quantity-by-holder of the US safe leg.
Fed S4-government issuer cells (domestic holder split of the safe leg).
USA issuer COLUMN of the CPIS grid (foreign portfolio holdings of US securities) as the broad-safe-leg quantity.
F2_treasury_despecialization.mapping_verdict: MAPPED ON THE QUANTITY SIDE ONLY. The convenience-yield/PRICE manifestation (kappa) is a yield observable NOT present in this stock/position matrix -> F2's price leg is a HOLE. F2 is identified only as a US-Treasury-specific QUANTITY factor. Consistent with old audit F2=IDENTIFIED(bounded; quantity-only).
F2_treasury_despecialization.separation_from_F4: F2 (US-Treasury-specific quantity move) and F4 (broad dollar run) both touch the USA column. They are separated by F2 loading on the TREASURY instrument (TIC I2 + Fed S4) while F4 loads on the BROAD dollar leg across instruments (CPIS total PI + BIS USD funding). The instrument dimension carries this separation; recorded as restriction R2 below.

F3_sanctions_reallocation.definition: Reallocation of claims ACROSS COUNTERPARTIES (issuer columns): holder i exits issuer j's claim and moves INTO issuer k's claim. A within-row substitution factor.
observed_quantities_it_loads_on: CPIS row-normalized issuer SHARES for each of the 13 reporter rows (s_split): the i->j vs i->k split, now OBSERVED across 13-14 issuer columns.

Specifically the offshore-hub destination columns CYM, HKG, VGB and the direct CHN column -- the cells US->CYM (2,798,130), US->HKG (107,448), US->VGB (51,419), US->CHN (255,341), and the analogous destination cells in other holders' rows.

Cross-holder consistency of the destination pattern: e.g. CYM as a destination column has 12 holders (col_holders), so 'reallocation into the China-conduit columns' is a pattern observable across holders, not just for the US row.
F3_sanctions_reallocation.mapping_verdict: MAPPED AT CELL LEVEL on the new support (REFUTES old audit 'reallocation axis absent'). BUT the ECONOMIC content of F3 -- reallocation toward CHINA-BY-NATIONALITY -- depends on attributing the offshore-hub columns (CYM/HKG/VGB) to issuer nationality, which is the BOUNDED HOLE (china_fraction_bound, [0,0.60]). So F3's CELLS are observed; F3's INTERPRETATION as China-reallocation is HOLE-dependent. STEP 3 sweeps exactly this.

F4_dollar_run.definition: Self-fulfilling run ACROSS HOLDERS on the dollar leg: a common, contagious propensity to exit dollar (USA-column) claims, propagating holder-to-holder -- NET of an official-sector backstop. F4 is a TWO-COMPONENT state (re-specified under official_sector_respec below): a run-DRAIN component (the common signed fall across the 12 USA-column holder cells, negative) AND a stress-activated official-sector backstop-INJECTION component of OPPOSITE SIGN (the Fed cross-border dollar provision via central-bank liquidity swap lines, which historically arrested the 2008 and 2020 runs, positive). The realized F4 stress dimension is the run drain operating against the official circuit-breaker, not the drain alone.
observed_quantities_it_loads_on: The USA issuer COLUMN of the CPIS grid across 12 holders: CYM->USA (4,513,338), IRL->USA (2,583,036), JPN->USA (2,342,597), LUX->USA (2,199,787), GBR->USA (1,824,619), DEU->USA (878,234), FRA->USA (876,069), NLD->USA (750,521), HKG->USA (419,578), CHN->USA (330,700), ITA->USA (238,397), BEL->USA (119,099). [RUN-DRAIN component, negative sign.]
BIS USD funding marginals as the contagion-conduit (common dollar-exit raises funding stress).
COFER world USD reserve-currency share (56.77%) as a single MARGINAL on currency substitution -- no holder resolution.

Fed central-bank-liquidity-swap outstanding balance (FRED SWPT / H.4.1 'Central bank liquidity swaps'), system aggregate, Millions USD, as the official-sector backstop-INJECTION cell of OPPOSITE (positive) sign. State-dependent: ~$35mn at the 2025 snapshot (calm), ~$583,135mn 2008-12-17 and ~$448,946mn 2020-05-27 (stress). SOURCE build/contracts/fed_swap_lines.json. See official_sector_respec below.
F4_dollar_run.mapping_verdict: CELLS MAPPED (the multi-holder dollar-leg block now EXISTS, refuting old audit 'holders are marginals only'). BUT identification of F4 AS A SEPARATE FACTOR is NOT automatic: on a SINGLE snapshot F4's cross-holder USA-column loading sits in the same rotation-indeterminate subspace as F1 (holder size/funding level) and F3 (the USA-column drop is the SOURCE of the within-row reallocation). Whether F4 is separately identified is the STEP-2 question; the cells existing is necessary, not sufficient. The backstop-injection component (official_sector_respec) adds a SECOND, opposite-sign leg to F4's measurement vector whose separate estimability is itself bounded (only 2008/2020 are backstop-active, single matrix snapshot) -- see official_sector_respec.estimability_bound.

official_sector_respec.motivation: Resolution Test C (build/audit/official_sector_test.json) established that the DP4 operator currently gives F4 a PURE draining -USAn block with NO circuit-breaker, while BOTH historical dollar-run realizations (2008, 2020) were ARRESTED by an opposite-sign Fed swap-line injection (+$583bn Dec-2008, +$449bn May-2020). Modeling the run without the official backstop models the disease without the intervention that defines the observed episodes and biases F4 run-feasibility upward. This sub-object re-specifies F4's INTERNAL measurement structure (run vs backstop); it does NOT alter the F3-vs-F4 separation (R_sep) or any other factor.
official_sector_respec.scope_statement: SPECIFICATION ONLY. No parameter is fit, no loading is scored, no hazard or date is produced here. Estimation/identification of this term is DP4/DP5, explicitly NOT in scope. This sub-object adds a state dimension and its restrictions; it does not estimate them.

claim_1_two_component_vF4.claim: v_F4 is re-specified as a TWO-COMPONENT measurement vector: v_F4 = [ d_run ; b_backstop ], where d_run is the run-DRAIN leg (common signed fall across the 12 CPIS USA-column holder cells) and b_backstop is the official-sector backstop-INJECTION leg (the SWPT/H.4.1 Fed dollar-provision outstanding balance entered as a single system-aggregate cell).
claim_1_two_component_vF4.sign_convention: Signs OPPOSE by construction and this is the load-bearing content. RUN: the USA-leg contracts -> d_run loads NEGATIVE on the USA-column holder cells (dollar exposure falls). BACKSTOP: official dollar provision EXPANDS under stress -> b_backstop loads POSITIVE on the Fed-provision/SWPT cell. A realized stress state is the negative drain partially or wholly offset by the positive injection; the two legs are not collinear because one is a private/portfolio cross-holder column move and the other is an official-sector balance-sheet expansion.
claim_1_two_component_vF4.genuine_state_not_cosmetic: b_backstop is STATE-DEPENDENT, not a fixed additive offset. It is ~0 in the calm state (SWPT ~$35mn at the 2025 snapshot) and ~$0.5tn-scale under stress (583,135mn 2008-12-17; 448,946mn 2020-05-27). It therefore enters as a REGIME/stress-activated component (active only when the swap facility is drawn), so it genuinely moves the F4 state under stress and does nothing in calm -- the opposite of a constant. A cosmetic constant would shift every state equally and could not arrest a run; a stress-activated opposite-sign term can.
claim_1_two_component_vF4.measurement_against_matrix: The backstop is measured as the SWPT/H.4.1 outstanding-swap balance occupying ONE official-sector cell: the Fed cross-border dollar-provision leg, a weekly STOCK/position of the same type the matrix records (Test C: the outstanding balance is a position, not merely a flow). Per build/contracts/fed_swap_lines.json the groundable series is a SYSTEM AGGREGATE only; therefore b_backstop enters as a single system-aggregate official-sector term. The per-counterparty-central-bank bilateral distribution (which foreign CB received the dollars -- ECB/BoJ/SNB/BoE/BoC) is an UNRESOLVED HOLE (UNVERIFIED in the contract: H.4.1 carries no counterparty split; NY Fed per-CB machine source not confirmable in environment). The bilateral distribution is FLAGGED as a hole, NOT invented.
claim_1_two_component_vF4.double_count_caution: The SWPT injection settles into foreign-bank dollar funding and overlaps conceptually with the BIS LBS USD bank-liability marginals that F4's contagion-conduit and F1 already load on. b_backstop must be wired so the same dollars are not counted both as the Fed-injection leg AND the BIS bank-liability leg. Recorded as a known modeling constraint for DP2/DP3 wiring; not resolved here.
claim_1_two_component_vF4.SUBJECT-DRIVER: F4 measurement vector v_F4 is driven by two opposite-sign legs: private cross-holder USA-column drain (d_run) and official Fed dollar-provision expansion (b_backstop).
claim_1_two_component_vF4.BOUNDARIES: b_backstop is a system-aggregate official cell only; per-CB bilateral split out of bounds (UNVERIFIED hole). Calm-state contribution is negligible (~$35mn); the term is material only in the stress regime.
claim_1_two_component_vF4.FALSIFIER: See claim_3. If the official-provision leg never loads opposite-sign to the drain on the backstop-active episodes, the two-component specification is wrong and v_F4 collapses to the single drain leg.
claim_1_two_component_vF4.SOURCE: build/contracts/fed_swap_lines.json (SWPT/H.4.1 series, weekly stock, Millions USD, peaks 583,135mn 2008-12-17 and 448,946mn 2020-05-27, current ~$35mn 2026-06-24, system-aggregate, per-CB UNVERIFIED); build/audit/official_sector_test.json (consequence analysis: mis_specifies_F4, opposite-sign injection, stock-not-flow, double-count caution, stress-state-only materiality).

claim_2_entry_into_global_games_selectio.claim: The official backstop is the CIRCUIT-BREAKER in the equilibrium-selection step: it raises the critical fundamental threshold theta*(global-games) at which the self-fulfilling run equilibrium becomes feasible, i.e. it can move the distance-to-frontier and prevent a run from ATTAINING the bad equilibrium even when fundamentals would otherwise admit it.
claim_2_entry_into_global_games_selectio.mechanism_consistent_with_equilibrium_co: In the Farhi-Maggiori multiple-equilibrium frame with Morris-Shin global-games selection already specified in step1_structural_model.equilibrium_concept, the run is selected when theta falls below theta*, and theta* rises with the strategic-complementarity strength among holders. A stress-activated official dollar injection WEAKENS the complementarity that drives the run (it supplies the dollars holders are scrambling for, lowering the payoff to exiting), which SHIFTS theta* so the run is feasible only at WORSE fundamentals. Operationally: b_backstop > 0 moves the selection boundary, increasing distance-to-frontier for a given theta. The backstop alters WHETHER a run REACHES the frontier, not the location of the fundamentals-gated manifold itself.
claim_2_entry_into_global_games_selectio.no_date_no_hazard: This is selection STRUCTURE only. No theta*, no distance-to-frontier value, and no hazard number is computed here (that is DP4/DP5). The breaking point remains the three objects (frontier, distance-to-frontier, hazard); the backstop enters as a shifter of the selection threshold, specified not located.
claim_2_entry_into_global_games_selectio.SUBJECT-DRIVER: Official backstop injection (b_backstop) drives an UPWARD shift in the global-games run-selection threshold theta*, via reduced cross-holder strategic complementarity.
claim_2_entry_into_global_games_selectio.BOUNDARIES: Effect is on equilibrium SELECTION (whether the run is reached), not on the fundamentals frontier's location. Magnitude of the threshold shift is unestimated (DP4/DP5). Holds only while the facility is active.
claim_2_entry_into_global_games_selectio.FALSIFIER: If, on a backstop-active episode, the run reaches the frontier DESPITE a large positive b_backstop (backstop insufficient), the threshold-shift is bounded/finite, not run-proof -- see claim_3. If the backstop never shifts the selection (run reaches frontier identically with and without injection), the circuit-breaker role is falsified.
claim_2_entry_into_global_games_selectio.SOURCE: build/audit/official_sector_test.json (2008/2020 runs arrested by opposite-sign injection; mis_specifies_F4 raises run-feasibility upward when backstop absent); step1_structural_model.equilibrium_concept (Farhi-Maggiori + global-games selection, theta* shifted by complementarity strength); build/contracts/fed_swap_lines.json (injection magnitudes).

claim_3_falsifier.claim: The two-component F4 re-spec is FALSIFIABLE because two discriminating cases predict DIFFERENT observable signatures in the matrix/operator: (A) backstop PRESENT but F4 still reaches the frontier (backstop INSUFFICIENT) versus (B) backstop ABSENT and F4 reaches the frontier.
claim_3_falsifier.case_A_backstop_present_but_insufficient: Operator signature: the SWPT/b_backstop cell carries a LARGE positive value (stress-scale, ~$0.5tn) co-timed with the negative USA-column drain, yet the run-equilibrium is still selected (distance-to-frontier crosses zero). In the matrix this looks like a big positive official-injection term that fails to offset the cross-holder drain. This is the 'circuit-breaker drawn but overwhelmed' case.
claim_3_falsifier.case_B_backstop_absent: Operator signature: the b_backstop cell is ~0 (facility not drawn) while the USA-column drain proceeds. In the matrix the run reaches the frontier with NO positive injection term present at all -- the 'no circuit-breaker' case.
claim_3_falsifier.why_distinguishable: Cases A and B differ in the VALUE of the b_backstop cell (large-positive vs ~zero) at the same drain configuration. A run that proceeds despite a large positive official-injection term is observationally distinct from a run with no injection term, so the specification is not decorative: the backstop leg has a concrete, non-degenerate footprint that the data can confirm or deny. If b_backstop could NOT distinguish these cases (e.g. it were a fixed constant), the term would be cosmetic and the spec would fail this test -- it is precisely the state-dependence that makes the falsifier bite.
claim_3_falsifier.what_failure_means: If on the backstop-active episodes (2008, 2020) the data show the run was NOT moderated by the positive injection leg (case A signature with no threshold shift), the circuit-breaker re-spec is rejected and F4 reverts to the pure-drain operator. The re-spec can therefore FAIL.
claim_3_falsifier.SUBJECT-DRIVER: Discriminating cases A (present-but-insufficient) vs B (absent) are driven by the VALUE of the b_backstop cell at a given drain, producing distinct operator signatures.
claim_3_falsifier.BOUNDARIES: Falsifier is evaluable only on episodes where the matrix/operator carries the b_backstop value; at the 2025 calm snapshot (~$35mn) neither case is active, so the falsifier is a STRESS-regime test, not a calm-snapshot test.
claim_3_falsifier.FALSIFIER: This claim IS the falsifier statement for the re-spec; its own meta-falsifier: if cases A and B produced identical operator signatures, the two-component spec would be non-falsifiable and must be rejected as cosmetic.
claim_3_falsifier.SOURCE: build/audit/official_sector_test.json (both 2008/2020 arrested -> backstop-present-and-sufficient was the realized case; the present-but-insufficient and absent cases are the unrealized discriminators); build/contracts/fed_swap_lines.json (stress vs calm magnitudes that populate the b_backstop cell).

estimability_bound.claim: Whether the backstop term is SEPARATELY ESTIMABLE is itself bounded and is NOT claimed to be identified here. With only TWO backstop-active episodes (2008, 2020) in the SWPT history and a SINGLE cross-sectional matrix snapshot (2025-S1, where SWPT ~$35mn), the b_backstop loading has at most two informative realizations and zero in-snapshot variation. Separate identification of the backstop leg from the run-drain leg is therefore data-limited and may not be achievable on the available support.
estimability_bound.honest_status: This sub-object SPECIFIES the backstop state and its restrictions; it does NOT assert the backstop term is identified. Whether b_backstop is separately estimable -- and whether the falsifier (claim_3) can actually be evaluated rather than merely stated -- is the DP5 identification-gate's job, NOT DP3's. DP3 hands a falsifiable two-component spec and its honest data-limitation bound to DP4/DP5; it does not pre-judge the gate.
estimability_bound.not_planted: The backstop is a FAITHFUL representation of an observed official mechanism that Test C grounded (the Fed swap line that arrested both historical runs), not a free parameter tuned to force an identification outcome. It is added because the mechanism is real and omitted, not to drive any conclusion. Its (non-)estimability is left as a genuine empirical question for the gate.
estimability_bound.SUBJECT-DRIVER: Separate estimability of b_backstop is driven (and bounded) by the count of backstop-active realizations (2: 2008, 2020) and the absence of in-snapshot variation (single 2025 matrix, SWPT near-zero).
estimability_bound.BOUNDARIES: At most two informative episodes; one calm snapshot; per-CB bilateral split UNVERIFIED. Estimability not asserted; identification verdict reserved to DP5.
estimability_bound.FALSIFIER: If DP5 finds b_backstop cannot be separated from d_run on this support, the backstop term is NOT IDENTIFIED -- a permitted and expected outcome under CLAUDE.md, recorded not manufactured around.
estimability_bound.SOURCE: build/contracts/fed_swap_lines.json (coverage 2002-2026 weekly; only 2008 and 2020 stress draws; current ~$35mn); build/audit/official_sector_test.json (structural/dynamics gap, stress-state-only materiality).

f_separability_untouched.claim: Adding the F4 official-sector backstop does NOT bear on, pin, or resolve the F3/F4 China-nationality-fraction (f) separability question. That question (china_fraction_bound [0,0.60], R_sep destination-exclusion truth-value) remains OPEN and UNTOUCHED.
f_separability_untouched.reasoning: This re-spec changes F4's INTERNAL structure (a run-drain leg plus an opposite-sign official backstop leg). It does NOT touch the F3-vs-F4 SEPARATION, which rests on the destination-exclusion restriction R_sep and its f-dependence. The backstop leg loads on the official-sector SWPT/H.4.1 cell, which is neither a CPIS USA-column drain cell nor a China-conduit destination column (CYM/HKG/VGB/CHN); it is orthogonal to the f-bearing destination block. Test C itself records forces_reopening_independent_of_f = true: the official-sector reopening is independent of f by construction. R_sep, R2, the equilibrium_concept, china_fraction_bound, and the f-sweep (dp3_sensitivity) are NOT modified by this sub-object.
f_separability_untouched.SUBJECT-DRIVER: The backstop leg loads on the official SWPT cell, disjoint from the f-bearing China-conduit destination columns, so it leaves R_sep's f-dependence unchanged.
f_separability_untouched.BOUNDARIES: Statement covers ONLY the F4 internal re-spec; it makes no claim about whether R_sep holds or fails across f.
f_separability_untouched.FALSIFIER: If the backstop cell were shown to share support with the China-conduit destination columns (it does not, per matrix cell registers), this independence claim would fail. No such shared support exists in the grounded sources.
f_separability_untouched.SOURCE: build/audit/official_sector_test.json (forces_reopening_independent_of_f = true); build/contracts/fed_swap_lines.json (backstop = official SWPT cell, not a CPIS destination column); step2_F3_F4_separating_restriction (R_sep destination-exclusion, left unmodified).

step2_F3_F4_separating_restriction.framing: F3 (across-issuer reallocation) and F4 (across-holder dollar run) both MOVE THE SAME CELLS: a fall in the USA column. The separation must be a TESTABLE overidentifying restriction, not an assertion of which is which. It is testable ONLY if it imposes more than it consumes given the single-snapshot covariance.

restriction_R_sep.name: DESTINATION-EXCLUSION restriction.
restriction_R_sep.economic_content: A sanctions reallocation (F3) is a SUBSTITUTION: a holder that exits issuer j moves the mass INTO a specific destination issuer k, holding its total portfolio size approximately fixed (re-balancing, not deleveraging). A dollar run (F4) is a COMMON FLIGHT: holders cut the USA/dollar leg TOGETHER and the mass leaves the measured dollar system -- into non-USD safe assets, gold, or out of the portfolio (deleveraging), WITHOUT a common compensating destination column inside the grid. The real-world mechanism that distinguishes them: F3 conserves the holder's row total and reallocates ACROSS columns; F4 shrinks the holder's USA exposure and is correlated ACROSS holders' USA cells with NO conserved row total.

matrix_variation_that_identifies_it.moves_under_F3_not_F4: The DESTINATION columns. F3 predicts that when a holder's USA share falls, a SPECIFIC destination column rises in the SAME row (within-row negative covariance: USA share down, China-conduit columns CYM/HKG/VGB/CHN up), and that this destination pattern is CONSISTENT across holders (a common destination loading). F4 predicts NO common in-grid destination -- the USA-column fall is NOT offset within the observed columns.
matrix_variation_that_identifies_it.moves_under_F4_not_F3: The CROSS-HOLDER co-movement of the USA column AT FIXED within-row split. F4 predicts the USA cells fall TOGETHER across holders proportional to each holder's dollar exposure, with the residual (non-USA) columns NOT systematically receiving the mass. F3 predicts NO purely-common USA-column move that is orthogonal to a destination rise.
matrix_variation_that_identifies_it.the_overidentifying_content: The restriction imposes that F3's loading vector is NONZERO on the destination columns (CYM/HKG/VGB/CHN) and ZERO on the pure-deleveraging direction, while F4's loading vector is NONZERO on the common USA-column direction and ZERO on the destination columns. That is 2 exclusion blocks on a single 13x14 covariance -- MORE restrictions than the 1 rotation angle needed to separate two factors in a 2D subspace, hence OVERIDENTIFYING and testable.

falsifier.statement: R_sep FAILS -- F3 and F4 become OBSERVATIONALLY EQUIVALENT on this matrix -- IF the destination columns that F3 reallocates INTO are themselves dollar-denominated / dollar-system claims that F4 would ALSO move, so that 'reallocate USA->destination' and 'run from the dollar' produce the SAME cell pattern. Concretely: if the China-conduit destination (CYM/HKG/VGB) is, by nationality, dominated by claims that are STILL on the US dollar leg (offshore USD issuance), then a fall in US->USA matched by a rise in US->CYM is NOT a move out of the dollar (F4 would not fire) NOR unambiguously a move toward China (F3's economic content), and the two factors load identically on the same cells. The single-snapshot worsens this: with ONE realization there is no time path to see whether the USA-column drop LEADS other holders' drops (contagion = F4) versus co-occurs with the same holder's own destination rise (substitution = F3).
falsifier.the_decisive_dependency: Whether the falsifier-case obtains DEPENDS ON the China-nationality fraction f of the offshore pool (china_fraction_bound [0,0.60]) AND on the currency composition of the destination columns. If the destination is genuinely China-by-nationality AND non-USD, F3 and F4 are distinguishable. If the destination is offshore-USD (US-bank ABS via Cayman SPVs -- explicitly flagged in china_fraction_bound footnote 3), the destination is still a dollar claim and the separation collapses.

does_falsifier_case_obtain_in_current_ma.verdict: PARTIALLY -- and the part that decides it is UNMEASURED. Matrix evidence: (1) The destination columns ARE populated and dense (CYM 12 holders, HKG 12, VGB 13, CHN 12), so the cell support for a destination-exclusion test EXISTS. (2) BUT the currency/nationality of the destination columns is RESIDENCY-tagged and NOT reallocated (matrix_spec offshore_hub_residency_status; dp2_residual gcap_residency_to_nationality = HOLE). The matrix CANNOT tell, from its own cells, whether US->CYM mass is China-by-nationality-non-USD (F3 destination, separation holds) or offshore-USD ABS (F4 also moves it, separation fails). (3) There is ONE time period, so the lead/lag contagion-vs-substitution discriminator is absent by construction.
does_falsifier_case_obtain_in_current_ma.consequence: On the CURRENT matrix the destination-exclusion restriction is EVALUABLE in form (the cells exist, unlike the old support where it was not even evaluable) but its truth-value is PINNED TO THE CHINA-NATIONALITY HOLE. So the F3/F4 separation is TESTABLE-IN-PRINCIPLE on this support (an advance over the old NOT-TESTABLE verdict) but its OUTCOME is conditional on f. Whether the conclusion is robust to f, or flips within [0,0.60], is computed in dp3_sensitivity.json (STEP 3). DP3 does NOT assert the separation; it specifies the test and hands the f-dependence to the sweep.

restriction_R2_F2_vs_F4.name: INSTRUMENT-EXCLUSION (separates F2 Treasury-quantity from F4 broad dollar run).
restriction_R2_F2_vs_F4.content: F2 loads on the TREASURY instrument leg (TIC I2 + Fed S4-government); F4 loads on the BROAD dollar leg across instruments (CPIS total PI USA column + BIS USD funding I1). Restriction: F2 loading is zero on non-Treasury USD funding; F4 loading is common across USD instruments.
restriction_R2_F2_vs_F4.falsifier: If the only multi-holder USD column in the matrix is the CPIS total-PI USA column (which COMBINES I2 debt + I3 equity, NOT split -- matrix_spec caveat 'P_TOTINV combines debt + equity; not split here'), then F2 (Treasury-debt-specific) and F4 (broad-dollar) project onto the SAME unsplit column and the instrument exclusion has no observable to bite on.
restriction_R2_F2_vs_F4.does_falsifier_obtain: YES, PARTIALLY. The CPIS USA column is instrument-unsplit (I2+I3 combined). The TIC column IS Treasury-only and single-debtor, and the Fed S4 cells ARE government-issuer, so a Treasury-vs-broad split EXISTS across the TIC/Fed layers but NOT within the CPIS multi-holder column. So F2/F4 separation is supported for the US-row Treasury quantity but THIN for the cross-holder dimension. Recorded as a bound, not a clean identification.

overidentifying_restrictions_of_three_ki.note: Per the DP3 mandate, the restriction set is of three kinds, each able to FAIL.

kind_1_rank_condition_on_systematic_cova.restriction: RANK(R1): the systematic (4-factor) part of the 13x14 CPIS covariance has rank EXACTLY 4 after removing the holder-size scale factor, with the F3 and F4 directions OCCUPYING DISTINCT left/right singular subspaces (F4 in the cross-holder/column-common subspace, F3 in the within-row/destination subspace).
kind_1_rank_condition_on_systematic_cova.how_it_can_fail: If the rank of the cross-holder-vs-destination block is < 2 (i.e. the USA-column-common direction and the destination-substitution direction are COLLINEAR on the single snapshot), then F3 and F4 are NOT separately spanned and the rank condition FAILS. This is checkable at DP4 by the singular spectrum of the (size-removed) grid. STATED so it can fail: it is NOT assumed to hold.
kind_1_rank_condition_on_systematic_cova.single_snapshot_caveat: Because there is ONE realization, 'covariance' here is the cross-sectional second moment of the single 13x14 grid (over its rows or columns), NOT a time-series covariance. The rank condition is therefore a statement about ONE matrix's singular structure; its failure mode (collinearity) is MORE likely on one snapshot than on a panel. This is a genuine vulnerability, recorded not hidden.

kind_2_theory_pinned_sign_pattern.restriction: SIGN(R_sep): F3's destination loadings on (CYM,HKG,VGB,CHN) are >= 0 (reallocation INTO the China-conduit, never out, under a sanctions-reallocation hypothesis); F4's USA-column loading is <= 0 across holders (a run REDUCES dollar exposure); and the F3 within-row total is conserved (sum of row-loadings ~ 0) while F4's is not (deleveraging).
kind_2_theory_pinned_sign_pattern.how_it_can_fail: If the fitted destination loadings come out with MIXED signs (some holders reallocate IN, others OUT, with no common direction), the theory-pinned sign pattern is violated and F3 is not the postulated common reallocation factor -- it would be noise. If F4's USA-column loadings are not common-signed across holders, the run factor is not a run. Checkable at DP4; able to fail.

kind_3_event_projection.restriction: EVENT(R_proj): named historical episodes project onto the factor they should load on, NOT onto the other. This is the planting guard made operational -- the assignment of episodes to F3 vs F4 is a TESTED restriction, not a premise.

episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.should_load_on: F3 (reallocation across counterparties: sanctioned holders shift OUT of US/EU claims INTO alternative counterparties).
episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.should_NOT_load_on: F4 (it is a targeted-counterparty event, not a system-wide dollar run).
episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.falsifier: If the 2022 episode's cell footprint loads on the COMMON USA-column direction (all holders cut USD together) rather than the destination-substitution direction, the F3 assignment is REJECTED and the episode belongs to F4 -- which would FLIP the binding-mode attribution of the hazard.

episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.should_load_on: F1 (common funding stress) and partially F4 (flight),
episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.should_NOT_load_on: F3 (it was not a counterparty-substitution event).
episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.falsifier: If 2008 loads on the destination-substitution direction, the F1/F4 vs F3 boundary is wrong.

episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.should_NOT_load_on: F4 (no broad cross-holder dollar exit occurred).
episode_assignments_to_be_TESTED_not_ass.falsifier: If this loads on the common cross-holder USA-column direction, F2 and F4 are not separated.

kind_3_event_projection.the_planting_test_made_explicit: ASK: if the 2022 episode were REASSIGNED from F3 to F4, would a downstream conclusion flip? YES -- it would move the breaking-point hazard's binding-mode from 'sanctions-reallocation pressure' to 'dollar-run pressure', changing whether the run equilibrium is judged feasible. THEREFORE this assignment is encoded as a TESTED restriction (EVENT R_proj above) with an explicit falsifier, NOT as a premise. F4 is NOT defined as 'the event that has not happened': F4 has a concrete observable footprint (common signed USA-column move across 12 holders) that the 2022 or any episode CAN load on, so its identification is an empirical question answerable in either direction.

step2_bottom_line.advance_over_old_audit: On the OLD one-column support the F3/F4 separating restriction was NOT EVALUABLE (no destination columns, no multi-holder block). On the NEW CPIS support the restriction R_sep is EVALUABLE IN FORM: the destination columns and the 12-holder dollar-leg block both EXIST as observed cells. This is a real change in identifiability status.
step2_bottom_line.the_remaining_obstruction: TWO obstructions remain, and they are NOT cured by adding CPIS: (1) the destination columns' issuer NATIONALITY and CURRENCY are a bounded HOLE (china_fraction_bound), so the destination-exclusion's TRUTH-VALUE is pinned to f in [0,0.60]; (2) the block is a SINGLE SNAPSHOT, so the contagion-vs-substitution (lead/lag) discriminator is absent and the rank condition is read off one matrix. F3/F4 separation is therefore TESTABLE-IN-PRINCIPLE but its OUTCOME is f-dependent and snapshot-fragile.
step2_bottom_line.honest_status_statement: F3/F4 separating restriction R_sep is SPECIFIED with a falsifier; the falsifier-case obtains PARTIALLY in the current matrix (destination cells exist but their nationality/currency is HOLE; single snapshot removes the dynamic discriminator). Whether the separation HOLDS or FAILS across the admissible China-nationality interval is resolved in dp3_sensitivity.json. DP3 does NOT manufacture a separation the data cannot support, and does NOT force 'now identified' merely because CPIS was added.

carried_forward_not_resolved: offshore-SPV issuer-nationality bounded HOLE (CYM/HKG/VGB portfolio pool); china_fraction_bound [0,0.60].
F2 convenience-yield/price leg (kappa): not in the stock matrix -> HOLE.
theta (issuer fundamental): out-of-matrix; required by the global-games selection; HOLE for this matrix.
single-snapshot: no time-series; contagion-vs-substitution dynamic discriminator absent.
